task.title: How to Take a Photo
task.shortdesc: After having your desired aperture and shutter speed settings, you are now ready to take photos!

step.cmd: Locate the Shutter Cocking Lever on the front of the camera and push it down to engage the Shutter Release.
Look through the View Finder on the rear of the camera and get your subject in general view.
Then look at your subject through the Range Finder and turn the Range Finder Dial until the two half-images form a complete image. This indicates you have the correct range.
Look again through the View Finder and compose your shot
Press the Shutter Release to take your photo.
To advance your film, hold down the Film Catch and turn the Film Wind Knob a half turn then release the film catch. Continue turning until the Film Wind Knob stops.

taskbody.result: The Film Counter Dial should now have advanced by one and the next photo is ready to be taken.Remember to advance your film after every shot so you don't risk taking a photo over the one you just took!
